inputs.name: securityProvider
inputs.description: Optional. The type of security provider. The valid values are: PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) JCE-KBE (Java Cryptography Extension-Key Based Encryption) The default value is PGP.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: The data that you want to decrypt must be in one of the following formats. If multiple input parameters are supplied for data , the service throws an exception stating that only one parameter must be passed.

inputs.name: loadAs
inputs.description: Optional. The format in which the service returns the output. Set to: bytes to return the output as a byte array. This is the default. stream to return the output as a stream object. string to return the output as a string.

inputs.name: secretKey
inputs.description: The secret key to extract the private key required to decrypt the data. For PGP, provide secretKeyBytes , secretKeyString , or secretKeyRingFile . If you provide secretKeyRingFile , you must also provide secretKeyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception. For JCE-KBE, provide secretKeyBytes , secretKeyString , or keyStoreAlias . If you provide keyStoreAlias , you must provide keyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception.

inputs.name: cipher
inputs.description: Optional. Applies only to JCE. The cipher for decryption. Select one of the following: RSA RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-256AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-512AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPPadding The default value is RSA.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted data as an output stream. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to stream .

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted data as bytes. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to bytes .

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted data as a string. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to string .

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Indicates whether the data is successfully decrypted or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: securityProvider
inputs.description: Optional. The type of security provider. The valid values are: PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) JCE-KBE (Java Cryptography Extension-Key Based Encryption) The default value is PGP.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: The data that you want to decrypt and verify must be in one of the following formats. If multiple input parameters are supplied for data , the service throws an exception stating that only one parameter must be passed.

inputs.name: loadAs
inputs.description: Optional The format in which the service returns the output. Set to: bytes to return the output as a byte array. This is the default. stream to return the output as a stream object. string to return the output as a string.

inputs.name: secretKey
inputs.description: The secret key to extract the private key required to decrypt the data. For PGP, provide secretKeyBytes , secretKeyString , or secretKeyRingFile . If you provide secretKeyRingFile , you must also provide secretKeyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception. For JCE-KBE, provide secretKeyBytes , secretKeyString , or keyStoreAlias . If you provide keyStoreAlias , you must provide keyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception.

inputs.name: publicKey
inputs.description: The public key required to verify the digital signature associated with the data. For PGP, provide publicKeyBytes , publicKeyString , or publicKeyRingFile . If you provide publicKeyRingFile , you must also provide publicKeyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception. For JCE-KBE, provide publicKeyBytes , publicKeyString , or truststoreAlias . If you provide truststoreAlias , you must also provide certAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception.

inputs.name: signingAlgorithm
inputs.description: The signing algorithm to use. For PGP, select one of the following: MD5 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 The default value is SHA256 for PGP. For JCE, select one of the following: RSA SHA256withRSA SHA512withRSA SHA3-256withRSA SHA3-384withRSA SHA3-512withRSA Note: If the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode is enabled, this service does not support the MD5 signing algorithm. FIPS mode can be enabled or disabled using the watt.security.fips.mode parameter. This is applicable only for PGP. Note: If the secret key for signing the data is of the DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm) key type, this service does not support the MD5 signing algorithm. This is applicable only for PGP.

inputs.name: cipher
inputs.description: String Optional. Applies only to JCE. The cipher for decryption. Select one of the following: RSA RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-256AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-512AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPPadding The default value is RSA.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted and verified data as an output stream. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to stream .

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted and verified data as bytes. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to bytes .

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted and verified data as a string. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to string .

outputs.name: verified
outputs.description: Indicates whether the signature associated with the data is verified or not. A value of: true indicates that the signed data is verified. false indicates that the signed data is not verified.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Indicates whether the data is successfully decrypted and verified. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: securityProvider
inputs.description: Optional. The type of security provider. The valid values are: PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) JCE-KBE (Java Cryptography Extension-Key Based Encryption) The default value is PGP.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Data that you want to encrypt. The data must be in one of the following formats. If multiple input parameters are supplied for data , the service throws an exception stating that only one parameter must be passed.

inputs.name: loadAs
inputs.description: Optional. The format in which the service returns the output. Set to: bytes to return the output as a byte array. This is the default. stream to return the output as a stream object. string to return the output as a string.

inputs.name: publicKey
inputs.description: The public key required to encrypt the data. For PGP, provide publicKeyBytes , publicKeyString , or publicKeyRingFile . If you provide publicKeyRingFile , you must also provide publicKeyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception. For JCE-KBE, provide publicKeyBytes , publicKeyString , or truststoreAlias . If you provide truststoreAlias , you must also provide certAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception.

inputs.name: encryption​Algorithm
inputs.description: The key encryption algorithm to use. For PGP, select one of the following symmetric key encryption algorithms: AES_192 AES_256 BLOWFISH IDEA TRIPLE_DES TWOFISH The default value is AES_256. For JCE, select RSA (asymmetric key encryption algorithm).

inputs.name: cipher
inputs.description: Optional. Applies only to JCE. The cipher for encryption. Select one of the following: RSA RSA/ECB/PKCS1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-256AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-512AndMGF1Padding RSA/ECB/OAEPPadding The default value is RSA.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: Conditional. Encrypted data as an output stream. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to stream .

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Conditional. Encrypted data as bytes. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to bytes .

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: Conditional. Encrypted data as a string in the ASCII-armored format. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to string .

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Indicates whether the data is successfully encrypted or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: inputString
inputs.description: The string that you want to convert to a hash value.

inputs.name: hashAlgorithm
inputs.description: Optional. The algorithm to generate the hash value. Select one of the following: MD5 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 The default value is SHA256.

inputs.name: saltValue
inputs.description: Optional. A random value that you can prefix to the input string before hashing. Note: To use a salt value in this service, run the pub.security:generateSalt service and map the generated salt value to this parameter.

outputs.name: hashedValue
outputs.description: Hashed value of the input string.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Indicates whether the inputString is successfully hashed or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: saltValue
inputs.description: Salt value to be used for hashing purposes.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Indicates whether a salt value is generated or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

outputs.name: saltValue
outputs.description: Salt value to be used for hashing purposes.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Indicates whether a salt value is generated or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: hostNames
inputs.description: Returns a list of IP addresses or hostnames in the CSV format, which can be allowed or denied IP access.

outputs.name: hostNames
outputs.description: Returns a list of IP addresses or hostnames in the CSV format, which can be allowed or denied IP access.

inputs.name: privKeyFile
inputs.description: Absolute (for example, D:\certs\cert1.der) or relative path of the file containing the private key. A relative path is the path relative to the directory from which the Integration Server has been started (for example, Integration Server_directory \instances\ instance_name \config\certs\cert1.der).

inputs.name: certFiles
inputs.description: of file names containing the certificates that comprise the certificate chain. The list should start with the user's certificate followed by (in order) intermediate certificates and the root CA certificate. Absolute or relative paths of the files can be specified.

inputs.name: privKey
inputs.description: A byte array containing the client's private key.

inputs.name: certs
inputs.description: List of byte arrays containing the client's certificate chain. The list should start with the user's certificate followed by (in sequence) intermediate certificates and the root CA certificate.

inputs.name: securityProvider
inputs.description: Optional. The type of security provider. The valid values are: PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) JCE-KBE (Java Cryptography Extension-Key Based Encryption) The default value is PGP.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Data that you want to sign. The data must be in one of the following formats. If multiple input parameters are supplied for data , the service throws an exception stating that only one parameter must be passed.

inputs.name: loadAs
inputs.description: Optional. The format in which the service returns the output. Set to: bytes to return the output as a byte array. This is the default. stream to return the output as a stream object. string to return the output as a string.

inputs.description: The secret key required to sign the data. For PGP, provide secretKeyBytes , secretKeyString , or secretKeyRingFile . If you provide secretKeyRingFile , you must also provide secretKeyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception. For JCE-KBE, provide secretKeyBytes , secretKeyString , or keyStoreAlias . If you provide keyStoreAlias , you must provide keyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception.

inputs.name: signingAlgorithm
inputs.description: The signing algorithm to use. For PGP, select one of the following: MD5 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 The default value is SHA256 for PGP. For JCE, select one of the following: RSA SHA256withRSA SHA512withRSA SHA3-256withRSA SHA3-384withRSA SHA3-512withRSA Note: If the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode is enabled, this service does not support the MD5 signing algorithm. FIPS mode can be enabled or disabled using the watt.security.fips.mode parameter. This is applicable only for PGP. Note: If the secret key for signing the data is of the DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm) key type, this service does not support the MD5 signing algorithm. This is applicable only for PGP.

outputs.description: Conditional. Signed data as an output stream. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to stream .

outputs.description: Indicates whether the data is successfully signed or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: userInfoClaims
inputs.description: Optional. A document (IData) containing the claims from the UserInfo token returned by the OpenID Provider’s UserInfo Endpoint.

inputs.name: userInfoError
inputs.description: Optional. A document (IData) containing the error when the OpenID Provider returns an unsuccessful response. Integration Server redirects the User Agent to the OpenID Provider’s Authorization Endpoint to authenticate the End User. If the OpenID Provider responds to that redirection with an error, Integration Server will populate error with the error information.

outputs.name: userInfoResponse
outputs.description: A document (IData) for the result of the UserInfo service.

inputs.name: securityProvider
inputs.description: Optional. The type of security provider. The valid values are: PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) JCE-KBE (Java Cryptography Extension-Key Based Encryption) The default value is PGP.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Signed data that you want to verify. The data must be in one of the following formats. If multiple input parameters are supplied for data , the service throws an exception stating that only one parameter must be passed.

inputs.name: loadAs
inputs.description: Optional. The format in which the service returns the output. Set to: bytes to return the output as a byte array. This is the default. stream to return the output as a stream object. string to return the output as a string.

inputs.name: publicKey
inputs.description: The signer's public key required to verify the signed data. For PGP, provide publicKeyBytes , publicKeyString , or publicKeyRingFile . If you provide publicKeyRingFile , you must also provide publicKeyAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception. For JCE-KBE, provide publicKeyBytes , publicKeyString , or truststoreAlias . If you provide truststoreAlias , you must also provide certAlias . Otherwise, the service throws an exception.

inputs.name: signingAlgorithm
inputs.description: The signing algorithm to use. For PGP, select one of the following: MD5 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 The default value is SHA256 for PGP. For JCE, select one of the following: RSA SHA256withRSA SHA512withRSA SHA3-256withRSA SHA3-384withRSA SHA3-512withRSA Note: If the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode is enabled, this service does not support the MD5 signing algorithm. FIPS mode can be enabled or disabled using the watt.security.fips.mode parameter. This is applicable only for PGP. Note: If the secret key for signing the data is of the DSA (Digital Signature Algorithm) key type, this service does not support the MD5 signing algorithm. This is applicable only for PGP.

inputs.name: actualData
inputs.description: Applies only to JCE. The original data that was signed and needs to be verified against the signature to confirm that it hasn't been tampered with. The actualData parameter must be in one of the specified formats. If multiple input parameters are provided for actualData , the service throws an exception indicating that only one parameter should be passed.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: Conditional. Verified data as an output stream. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to stream .

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: Conditional. Verified data in bytes. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to bytes .

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: Conditional. Verified data as a string. Returned when the loadAs input parameter is set to string .

outputs.name: verified
outputs.description: Indicates whether the signature associated with the data is valid or not. A value of: true indicates that the signed data is verified. false indicates that the signed data is not verified.

outputs.name: status
outputs.description: Indicates whether the data is successfully verified or not. If successful, status is success . Otherwise, status contains failure along with an error message.

inputs.name: ruleName
inputs.description: The Enterprise Gateway rule that the request violates.

inputs.name: alertInfo
inputs.description: Information for Enterprise Gateway alert notification.

inputs.name: requestHeaders
inputs.description: IData object containing the standard HTTP headers in the request along with any custom headers that are present in the request. The following are the standard headers that will be extracted from the request: User-Agent , Accept , Host , Authorization , Cookie , Content-Type , Accept-Language , and Content-Length .

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: Object containing the payload in the request.

outputs.name: forwardRequest
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether Enterprise Gateway Server forwards the request to the Internal Server. A value of: True indicates that the request will be forwarded to the Internal Server. False indicates that the request will be denied and an error message is returned to the user.

inputs.name: signerInfo
inputs.description: Information about a single signer of the signed data object. Each signerInfo requires either a certificate chain and a private key or a key alias that references them.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Optional. Data in the form of a byte array to be digitally signed. Note: If both data and dataAsStream are provided, dataAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: dataAsStream
inputs.description: java.io.InputStream Optional. Data in the form of an input stream to be digitally signed. Note: If both data and dataAsStream are provided, dataAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: detachedSignature
inputs.description: Flag specifying whether to generate a detached signature. A detached signature does not include the data that was signed. Set to: true to generate a detached signature. false to generate an implicit signature (one that includes the signed data). This is the default.

outputs.name: signature
outputs.description: Conditional. Signature generated in the form of a byte array from the supplied data. This is a DER-encoded representation of the SignedData object as specified in PKCS#7. signature is returned when the input parameter data is provided.

outputs.name: signatureAsStream
outputs.description: java.io.OutputStream Conditional. Signature generated in the form of an output stream from the supplied data. signatureAsStream is returned when the input parameter dataAsStream is provided.

inputs.name: trustStoreAlias
inputs.description: Alias for the truststore containing the certificate.

inputs.name: certAlias
inputs.description: Alias identifying a particular trusted certificate within a truststore.

outputs.name: certificate
outputs.description: A byte array containing the trusted certificate.

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Alias for the keystore that contains the private key of interest and its certificate.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Alias for the private key stored in the specified keystore.

outputs.name: privateKey
outputs.description: Object representing the private key.

outputs.name: certChain
outputs.description: List of byte arrays representing the certificate chain associated with the private key.

inputs.name: trustStoreAlias
inputs.description: Name of the truststore alias.

outputs.name: certificates
outputs.description: Trusted certificates, as a list of byte arrays.

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Name of the keystore alias.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the private key located in the keystore.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: Key of the password entry to be retrieved.

inputs.name: isInternal
inputs.description: "true" if this is an internal password; "false" if it is public. By default, this is "false". If you specify incorrectly whether the password is internal or public, the retrieve operation will fail. (For more information about internal and public passwords, see Internal and Public Passwords Internal passwords are passwords for use by the Integration Server itself to access secure resources (e.g., remote Integration Servers, JDBC connection pools, LDAP servers, etc.). Internal passwords are managed using the Integration Server Administrator and are stored in the outbound password store. Flow services are also allowed to store passwords in the outbound password store. However, by default, passwords stored by a flow service are considered "public," as opposed to internal. This distinction allows flow services to use the outbound password store as a secure mechanism for storing and retrieving passwords, but protects the Integration Server's internal passwords. When calling any of the pub.security.outboundPasswords services (i.e. setPassword, getPassword, listKeys, removePassword, and updatePassword) the isInternal input parameter indicates whether the service is working with internal or public passwords. Note that even if this parameter is set to "true", you cannot access internal passwords if the Integration Server is configured to deny access to internal passwords. Access to internal passwords is controlled by the watt.security.ope.AllowInternalPasswordAccess configuration parameter on the Integration Server; for more information see webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide. .)

outputs.name: value
outputs.description: Value of the retrieved password.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: "true" if the password value was successfully retrieved; "false" otherwise.

outputs.name: message
outputs.description: "successful" or reason for failure.

inputs.name: isInternal
inputs.description: "true" if you want keys for internal passwords; "false" if you want keys for public passwords. By default this is "false". (For more information about internal and public passwords, see Internal and Public Passwords Internal passwords are passwords for use by the Integration Server itself to access secure resources (e.g., remote Integration Servers, JDBC connection pools, LDAP servers, etc.). Internal passwords are managed using the Integration Server Administrator and are stored in the outbound password store. Flow services are also allowed to store passwords in the outbound password store. However, by default, passwords stored by a flow service are considered "public," as opposed to internal. This distinction allows flow services to use the outbound password store as a secure mechanism for storing and retrieving passwords, but protects the Integration Server's internal passwords. When calling any of the pub.security.outboundPasswords services (i.e. setPassword, getPassword, listKeys, removePassword, and updatePassword) the isInternal input parameter indicates whether the service is working with internal or public passwords. Note that even if this parameter is set to "true", you cannot access internal passwords if the Integration Server is configured to deny access to internal passwords. Access to internal passwords is controlled by the watt.security.ope.AllowInternalPasswordAccess configuration parameter on the Integration Server; for more information see webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide. .)

outputs.name: key
outputs.description: List of keys in the password store.

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: "true" if the list of keys was successfully retrieved; "false" otherwise.

inputs.name: key
inputs.description: Key to be associated with the password entry.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Password to be stored.

inputs.name: isInternal
inputs.description: "true" if this should be saved as an internal password; "false" if it should be saved as a public password. Default is "false". (See Internal and Public Passwords Internal passwords are passwords for use by the Integration Server itself to access secure resources (e.g., remote Integration Servers, JDBC connection pools, LDAP servers, etc.). Internal passwords are managed using the Integration Server Administrator and are stored in the outbound password store. Flow services are also allowed to store passwords in the outbound password store. However, by default, passwords stored by a flow service are considered "public," as opposed to internal. This distinction allows flow services to use the outbound password store as a secure mechanism for storing and retrieving passwords, but protects the Integration Server's internal passwords. When calling any of the pub.security.outboundPasswords services (i.e. setPassword, getPassword, listKeys, removePassword, and updatePassword) the isInternal input parameter indicates whether the service is working with internal or public passwords. Note that even if this parameter is set to "true", you cannot access internal passwords if the Integration Server is configured to deny access to internal passwords. Access to internal passwords is controlled by the watt.security.ope.AllowInternalPasswordAccess configuration parameter on the Integration Server; for more information see webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide. for more information.)

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: "true" if password was successfully stored; "false" otherwise.

outputs.name: message
outputs.description: "successful" or reason for failure.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Data to be digitally signed.

inputs.name: detachedSignature
inputs.description: Flag specifying whether to generate a detached signature. A detached signature does not include the data that was signed. Set to: true to generate a detached signature. false to generate an implicit signature (one that includes the signed data). This is the default.

outputs.name: signature
outputs.description: Signature generated from the supplied data. This is a DER-encoded representation of the SignedData object as specified in PKCS#7.

inputs.name: signature
inputs.description: Optional. Signature to use to determine whether the signed data is intact (a DER-encoded representation of the SignedData object as specified in PKCS#7) where the signature is in the form of a byte array. If you are processing a detached signature, pass the signature in signature . If you are processing an implicit signature, pass the entire signed message in signature . Note: If both signature and signatureAsStream are provided, signatureAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: signatureAsStream
inputs.description: java.io.InputStream Optional. Signature to use to determine whether the signed data is intact where the signature is in the form of an input stream. Note: If both signature and signatureAsStream are provided, signatureAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: Optional. Data in the form of a byte array that was signed. If you are processing a detached signature, you must supply data . If you are only processing an implicitly signed data as a byte array, you do not need to supply data because both the data and the signature reside in signature . If you are processing a detached signature and an implicitly signed data as a byte array, you must supply data . Note: If both data and dataAsStream are provided, dataAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: dataAsStream
inputs.description: java.io.InputStream Optional. Data in the form of an input stream that was signed. If you are processing a detached signature, you must supply dataAsStream . If you are only processing an implicitly signed data as an input stream, you do not need to supply dataAsStream because both the data and the signature reside in signatureAsStream . If you are processing a detached signature and an implicitly signed data as an input stream, you must supply dataAsStream . Note: If both data and dataAsStream are provided, dataAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: detachedSignature
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether the message has a detached signature. Set to: true when the message has a detached signature. false when the message has an implicit signature. This is the default. Note: If detachedSignature is true and signature is provided, then data should be provided. If detachedSignature is true and signatureAsStream is provided, then dataAsStream should be provided.

inputs.name: signerCertChain
inputs.description: Optional. Certificate chains of the parties that signed the message. Note: If the signers included the certificate chain with the digital signature, you do not need to supply signerCertChain .

outputs.name: content
outputs.description: Conditional. The data (for example, the document that was originally signed) extracted in the form of a byte array from an implicit signature. If you are verifying a detached signature, content is not returned. Note: The extracted data is returned in content even if signature verification fails. content is returned when the input parameter data is provided.

outputs.name: contentAsStream
outputs.description: java.io.OutputStream Conditional. The data extracted in the form of an output stream from an implicit signature. contentAsStream is returned when the input parameter dataAsStream is provided.

outputs.name: signerInfo
outputs.description: Information about the signers. Each document in the list provides the following information about a single signer:

inputs.name: secureString
inputs.description: WmSecureString to be converted.

inputs.name: returnAs
inputs.description: Format into which the WmSecureString is to be converted. Valid options are byte[], char[], and Java String. If a value for this parameter is not specified, the default is to convert the WmSecureString to a String.

outputs.name: string
outputs.description: The WmSecureString converted to a Java String.

outputs.name: bytes
outputs.description: The WmSecureString converted to a native Java byte array.

outputs.name: chars
outputs.description: The WmSecureString converted to a native Java character array.

inputs.name: algorithm
inputs.description: Name of the algorithm that you want to use to compute the message digest. Must be one of the following: MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, or SHA-512 .

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Optional. Message for which you want the digest generated where the message is in the form of a byte array. Note: If both input and inputAsStream are provided, inputAsStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: inputAsStream
inputs.description: java.io.InputStream Optional. Message for which you want to generate a message digest where the message is in the form of an input stream. Note: If both input and inputAsStream are provided, inputAsStream takes precedence.

outputs.name: output
outputs.description: Conditional. Computed digest in the form of a byte array. output is returned when the input parameter input is provided.

outputs.name: outputAsStream
outputs.description: OutputStream Conditional. Computed digest in the form of an output stream. outputAsStream is returned when the input parameter inputAsStream is provided.

inputs.name: string
inputs.description: Java String to made into a WmSecureString.

inputs.name: bytes
inputs.description: Byte array to be made into a WmSecureString.

inputs.name: chars
inputs.description: Character array to be made into a WmSecureString.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: If a byte array is supplied as an input parameter, encoding specifies the Java encoding of the byte array. This may be any encoding supported by Java String. By default, if no encoding is specified, then the default JVM encoding is used.

outputs.name: secureString
outputs.description: WmSecureString created from the supplied input parameters.

inputs.name: secureString
inputs.description: WmSecureString to be destroyed.

inputs.name: certificate
inputs.description: The certificate whose information you want to retrieve.

inputs.name: dateFormat
inputs.description: Optional. The date format to use for the date returned by the notBefore and notAfter fields in the validity output parameter. The value of dateFormat must be a pattern used by java.text.SimpleDateFormat , for example: MM/dd/yyyy By default, the pub.security.util:getCertificateInfo service uses a preset date format that returns only the last two digits of the certificate expiration year. This can lead can lead to ambiguity as a certificate that expires in 2109 returns an expiration year of 09, which some systems might treat as 2009.

outputs.name: info
outputs.description: Information from the certificate.

inputs.name: certificateChain
inputs.description: The certificate chain in PKCS #7 format.

outputs.name: certificates
outputs.description: List of byte arrays in which each byte[ ] in the list contains a certificate from certificateChain .

inputs.name: xmldata
inputs.description: Optional. Encrypted XML that needs to be decrypted as plain text.

inputs.name: xmlStream
inputs.description: Optional. Encrypted XML in the form of an input stream. Note: If both xmldata and xmlStream are provided, xmlStream takes precedence; Integration Server uses the xmlStream value and returns only decryptedXMLStream .

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Optional. Alias of the keystore that contains the private key used for decryption.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Optional. Alias of the private key, contained in the keystore specified by the keyStoreAlias parameter, that is used for decryption.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the encoding to use if the encoding cannot be extracted from the XML. If encoding is not specified in the XML document or in the encoding parameter, Integration Server uses UTF-8. The encoding value must be a valid IANA encoding.

outputs.name: decryptedXMLData
outputs.description: Conditional. Decrypted XML data. decryptedXMLData is returned when the input parameter xmldata is provided.

outputs.name: decryptedXMLStream
outputs.description: Conditional. A decrypted XML OutputStream object. decryptedXMLStream is returned when the input parameter xmlStream is provided.

inputs.name: xmldata
inputs.description: Optional. The XML to be encrypted.

inputs.name: xmlStream
inputs.description: Optional. Input stream to the XML that needs to be encrypted. Note: If both xmldata and xmlStream are provided, xmlStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: nodeSelectors
inputs.description: XPaths to the node to be encrypted. If the value for this parameter is left empty, no XML will be encrypted.

inputs.name: nsDecls
inputs.description: Optional. Mapping of the namespace prefixes to the namespace URIs. The first column contains the prefixes and the second column contains the corresponding URIs.

inputs.name: recipientID
inputs.description: Optional. Name of the client to which the XML will be sent. The user name and certificate must be configured with Integration Server certificate mapping. The client name entry is mapped to a valid X.509 certificate, and both are stored in Integration Server . For information about Integration Server certificate mapping, see IBM webMethods Integration Server Administrator’s Guide .

inputs.name: recipientCert
inputs.description: Optional. The certificate containing the public key that will be used to encrypt the XML. If the input parameters recipientCert and recipientID are both provided, recipientCert is used.

inputs.name: contentOnly
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the XML tags surrounding the content will be encrypted along with the content. Set to: true to encrypt only the content. false to encrypt both the tags and the content. This is the default.

inputs.name: algorithm
inputs.description: Optional. The symmetric key algorithm to use for encryption. Set to: tripledes-cbc for the algorithm at http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#tripledes-cbc This is the default. aes256-cbc for the algorithm at http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#aes256-cbc aes192-cbc for the algorithm at http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#aes192-cbc aes128-cbc for the algorithm at http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#aes128-cbc Note: If you are using aes256-cbc or aes192-cbc with JVM 1.6, make sure the unlimited policy jar files have been installed.

inputs.name: encryptedKeyAlgorithm
inputs.description: Optional. The symmetric key that is randomly generated, and then encrypted with the receiver's public key. This encryption uses an asymmetric algorithm if public/private key pairs are being used. Set to: rsa-1_5 for the algorithm at http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-1_5 This is the default. rsa-oaep-mgf1p for the algorithm at http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-oaep-mgf1p

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the encoding to use if the encoding cannot be extracted from the XML. If encoding is not specified in the XML document or in the encoding parameter, Integration Server uses UTF-8. The encoding value must be a valid IANA encoding.

outputs.name: encryptedXMLData
outputs.description: Conditional. Encrypted XML data. encryptedXMLData is returned when the input parameter xmldata is provided.

outputs.name: encryptedXMLStream
outputs.description: Conditional. Encrypted XML in the form of an OutputSream. encryptedXMLStream is returned when the input parameter xmlStream is provided.

inputs.name: xmldata
inputs.description: Optional. XML that needs to be signed.

inputs.name: xmlStream
inputs.description: Optional. Input stream containing the XML that needs to be signed. Note: If both xmldata and xmlStream are provided, xmlStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: uri
inputs.description: Optional. URI to the element to be signed. In combination with the nodeSelectors parameter, the uri identifies the nodes to be signed.

inputs.name: noNamespace SchemaLocation
inputs.description: Optional. A URI that identifies the location of the XML schema definition that contains the ID attribute specified in uri . Provide a noNamespaceSchemaLocation when specifying an ID attribute for uri and the ID attribute resides in an XML schema with no namespaces.

inputs.name: schemaLocations
inputs.description: Optional. Document (IData) containing name-value pairs for the XML namespace and the location of the XML schema definition that contains element declarations, attribute declarations, and type definitions for that namespace. Provide a schemaLocation when specifying an ID attribute for uri and the ID attribute resides in an XML schema for a particular namespace. For example, XML namespace = http://www.w3schools.com XML schema definition location = file:C:/note.xsd

inputs.name: nodeSelectors
inputs.description: XPath notation that identifies the nodes to be signed. The locations of the XPaths are not absolute, but relative, and work within the context of the node (an XPath Axes). Important: Do not use absolute location XPaths here.

inputs.name: nsDecls
inputs.description: Optional. Mapping of the namespace prefixes to the namespace URIs. The first column contains the prefixes and the second column contains the corresponding URIs.

inputs.name: isEnveloped
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the signature is enveloped or enveloping. Set to: True to indicate that the generated signature will be enveloped. This is the default. False to indicate that the generated signature will be enveloping, provided the isDetached parameter is set to False . Unlike the detached signature, which is kept apart from the original document, enveloping and enveloped signatures are tightly coupled with the original document.

inputs.name: isDetached
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the signature is detached or not. Integration Server uses <detached-signature> as the container for signature and the XML to be signed. Set to: True to indicate that the generated signature will be detached. False to indicate that the generated signature will be enveloping or enveloped, based on the isEnveloped parameter setting. This is the default. Note: Setting isDetached to True and isEnveloped to False , indicates that the signature will be detached. Setting isDetached to False and isEnveloped to True , indicates that the signature will be enveloped. Setting isDetached to False and isEnveloped to False , indicates that the signature will be enveloping. Setting isDetached to True and isEnveloped to True , results in error.

inputs.name: signatureNodeSelector
inputs.description: Optional. XPath to the node where the signature is entered. Applicable only for enveloped signatures. If no value is provided, the signature is placed as a first child of the root node.

inputs.name: signatureAlgorithm
inputs.description: Optional. Signature algorithm to use when signing the XML node or document. Specify one of the following or use the default value (first algorithm): SHA1 (default) SHA256 SHA384 SHA512

inputs.name: digestAlgorithm
inputs.description: Optional. Digest algorithm to use when signing the XML node or document. Specify one of the following or use the default value (first algorithm): SHA1 (default) SHA256 SHA384 SHA512

inputs.name: canonicalizationAlgorithm
inputs.description: Optional. Canonical algorithm used with the XML. Specify one of the following or use the default value (first algorithm): http://www.w3.org/TR/2001/rec-xml-c14n-20010315 (default) http://www.w3.org/TR/2001/rec-xml-c14n-20010315#WithComments http://www.w3.org/2001/10/xml-exc-c14n# http://www.w3.org/2001/10/xml-exc-c14n#WithComments

inputs.name: signatureId
inputs.description: Optional. ID attribute for the signature node.

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Optional. Name (alias) of the keystore that contains the private key/certificate.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Optional. Name (alias) of the private key, contained in the keystore specified by the keyStoreAlias parameter, that is used for signing.

inputs.name: keyName
inputs.description: Optional. Name that is used by the signer to communicate a key identifier to the recipient.

inputs.name: includeKeyValue
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether to include the key value (RSAKeyValue or DSAKeyValue), based on the certificate used for signing. The KeyValue element contains a single public key that can be used in validating the signature. Set to: True to include the key value (RSAKeyValue or DSAKeyValue) based on the certificate used for signing. False to exclude the key value from the signature.

inputs.name: includeCertChain
inputs.description: Optional. Indicates whether the certificate chain should be included in the signature. Set to: True to include the certificate chain in the signature. False to leave the certificate chain out of the signature. This is the default.

inputs.name: certData
inputs.description: Optional. Select the X509 certificate data to be entered into the signature's key information. X509_CERTIFICATE (default) X509_SUBJECT_NAME X509_ISSUER_SERIAL X509_SKI X509_CRL Note that the initials "SKI" and "CRL"in denote "Subject Key Identifier" and Certificate Revocation List".

inputs.name: idXmlObject
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the ID for the node that holds the original XML that is signed. Applicable only for enveloping signatures.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the encoding to use if the encoding cannot be extracted from the XML. If encoding is not specified in the XML document or in the encoding parameter, Integration Server uses UTF-8. The encoding value must be a valid IANA encoding.

inputs.name: addSignatureAsLastElement
inputs.description: Optional. When isEnveloped is set to True , this parameter indicates the position at which Integration Server should add the signature element child to the root. Set to: True to add the signature element as the last child of the root. False to add the signature element as the first child of the root. This is the default.

inputs.name: addDSPrefixToSignature
inputs.description: Optional. Set to False to create a Signature element in the signed XML without any prefixes. By default, this input variable is set to True and the "ds:" prefix is added.

outputs.name: signedXMLData
outputs.description: Conditional. Signed XML data. signedXMLData is returned when xmlData is provided.

outputs.name: signedXMLStream
outputs.description: Conditional. Signed XML in the form of an OutputSream. signedXMLStream is returned when xmlStream is provided.

inputs.name: xmldata
inputs.description: Optional. Signed XML that needs to be verified.

inputs.name: xmlStream
inputs.description: Optional. Signed XML as an input stream that needs to be verified. Note: If both xmldata and xmlStream are provided, xmlStream takes precedence.

inputs.name: signatureSelectors
inputs.description: XPaths that are used to identify the signature; can be any valid XPath. Following is an example: //*[@ID="Sign001"]

inputs.name: nsDecls
inputs.description: Optional. Mapping of the namespace prefixes to the namespace URIs. The first column contains the prefixes and the second column contains the corresponding URIs.

inputs.name: noNamespace SchemaLocation
inputs.description: Optional. Schema location for elements with no namespace. This parameter is used to locate the schema that defines elements without a namespace prefix.

inputs.name: schemaLocations
inputs.description: Optional. Holds the schema locations against the namespaces.

inputs.name: encoding
inputs.description: Optional. Specifies the encoding to use if the encoding cannot be extracted from the XML. If encoding is not specified in the XML document or in the encoding parameter, Integration Server uses UTF-8. The encoding value must be a valid IANA encoding.

outputs.name: verifcationResult
outputs.description: Indicates whether the signed XML is authentic (true) or cannot be verified or shows signs of tampering (false).

outputs.name: failedSignatureSelector
outputs.description: Conditional. In case of a verification failure (the digests do not equate), indicates which signature selector failed.

outputs.name: failureReason
outputs.description: Conditional. This output parameter is populated only in the case of a verification failure. Its value indicates whether (1) the signature caused the failure, or (2) the signature is from an untrusted certificate. Possible values are: SIGNATURE FAILED CERTIFICATE NOT TRUSTED

outputs.name: certMap
outputs.description: Conditional. For each XPath in signatureSelector , certMap contains a document that identifies the XPath and the corresponding signing certificate found at that XPath. certMap is only returned if certificates were resolved for at least one the XPaths specified in signatureSelector . If Integration Server encounters a signature failure, Integration Server does not resolve any subsequent XPaths. certMap contains all of the XPaths and corresponding certificates that Integration Server could resolve up to the point of failure.
